title: tests: update CI images
body:
preparation work for: https://github.com/containers/common/pull/573

Signed-off-by: Giuseppe Scrivano <gscrivan@redhat.com>

comments.body:

we need an updated runc.  v1.0-rc95 has the feature we need
@cevich PTAL

> we need an updated runc. v1.0-rc95 has the feature we need

Sorry only just seeing this now after being on PTO last week.  Okay, I'll build new VM images...[okay, opened a PR.](https://github.com/containers/automation_images/pull/72)

@giuseppe okay, give image suffix `c6704734020042752` a try.

```
$ podman run -it --rm quay.io/libpod/fedora_podman:c6704734020042752 rpm -q runc
Trying to pull quay.io/libpod/fedora_podman:c6704734020042752...

```
@giuseppe needs a rebase.  @cevich I think we need new VMs to get latest runc.
I am hitting https://bugzilla.redhat.com/show_bug.cgi?id=1965743 now, we will need new images once that is fixed

> I am hitting https://bugzilla.redhat.com/show_bug.cgi?id=1965743 now

Ugh, that's frustrating.  If it ain't one thing...it's another thing :cry: 

> we will need new images once that is fixed

Sure thing, happy to.  I left open [the image-build PR](https://github.com/containers/automation_images/pull/72) so when it's time, poke me and I can smash the re-run button.  That way the image-id won't change, it'll rebuild and overwrite.

the `cri-o-runc` deb package on ubuntu:2010 is still too old, and I think it is also vulnerable to the last runc security issue.

@lsm5 FYI

```
$ podman run --rm quay.io/libpod/prior-ubuntu_podman:c6704734020042752 dpkg-query -s cri-o-runc
Package: cri-o-runc
Status: install ok installed
Priority: extra
Section: devel
Installed-Size: 11743
Maintainer: Lokesh Mandvekar <lsm5@fedoraproject.org>
Architecture: amd64
Version: 1.0.0~rc93.1
Depends: libc6 (>= 2.28), libseccomp2 (>= 2.4.1)
Breaks: docker.io (<< 1.12)
Description: Open Container Project - runtime
 "runc" is a command line client for running applications packaged according
 to the Open Container Format (OCF) and is a compliant implementation of
 the Open Container Project specification.
 This package is a fork of the "runc' package, specifically for cri-o.
Homepage: https://github.com/opencontainers/runc

```
you may want an apt update, I updated to rc95 the other day: https://build.opensuse.org/package/show/devel:kubic:libcontainers:stable/cri-o-runc

Thanks but I think we are not using that repository, as I don't see any possible upgrade.

@cevich do you think we can move to using that packages for our images?

> Thanks but I think we are not using that repository, as I don't see any possible upgrade.

Yep, you got it.

> you may want an apt update, I updated to rc95 the other day:

We have a special 'testing' kubik repo. @lsm5 made just for CI. It's *suppose* to be even more bleeding edge.  @haircommander do you know how to check if maybe the build failed or never happened in that repo?
oh it definitely never happened. lemme see how easy it'll be to mirror my changes
builds are in the oven now :bread:

Oh goody, I'm glad you could figure it out.

@lsm5 - un-ping :smile:
the failures don't seem related to the seccomp change
@giuseppe needs a rebase.

@adrianreber it looks like some checkpoint tests are failing with runc-1.0-rc95 on Ubuntu:

```
[+1588s] Running: /var/tmp/go/src/github.com/containers/podman/bin/podman --storage-opt vfs.imagestore=/tmp/podman/imagecachedir --root /tmp/podman_test174508096/crio --runroot /tmp/podman_test174508096/crio-run --runtime /usr/lib/cri-o-runc/sbin/runc --conmon /usr/libexec/podman/conmon --cni-config-dir /etc/cni/net.d --cgroup-manager systemd --tmpdir /tmp/podman_test174508096 --events-backend file --storage-driver vfs container checkpoint --with-previous d634e5cabd4879629628bb0b1775dce9c701f79ae2a63acf4f740d288f5cee68
[+1588s] time="2021-06-10T11:08:49Z" level=error msg="--parent-path must be relative"
[+1588s] Error: `/usr/lib/cri-o-runc/sbin/runc checkpoint --image-path /tmp/podman_test174508096/crio/vfs-containers/d634e5cabd4879629628bb0b1775dce9c701f79ae2a63acf4f740d288f5cee68/userdata/checkpoint --work-path /tmp/podman_test174508096/crio/vfs-containers/d634e5cabd4879629628bb0b1775dce9c701f79ae2a63acf4f740d288f5cee68/userdata --parent-path /tmp/podman_test174508096/crio/vfs-containers/d634e5cabd4879629628bb0b1775dce9c701f79ae2a63acf4f740d288f5cee68/userdata/pre-checkpoint d634e5cabd4879629628bb0b1775dce9c701f79ae2a63acf4f740d288f5cee68` failed: exit status 1
```

Not sure if it is a regression in runc, or an issue in Podman

That is from a fix in runc: https://github.com/opencontainers/runc/pull/2913

pre-checkpointing only works correctly with a relative path

The reason why it did not fail before is that CRIU used to silently ignore if the parent path was not a relative path. CRIU just switches to non-precheckpointing mode. So the test was definitely always wrong in Podman. At least it was not doing what it was supposed.

Newer versions of CRIU (unreleased) will fail. There was also a check introduced in runc which is why this breaks now here.

Looking how to fix it now.

This boils down to something like this:
```
diff --git a/libpod/oci_conmon_linux.go b/libpod/oci_conmon_linux.go
index 3da49b85f..58e0a284b 100644
--- a/libpod/oci_conmon_linux.go
+++ b/libpod/oci_conmon_linux.go
@@ -760,7 +760,7 @@ func (r *ConmonOCIRuntime) CheckpointContainer(ctr *Container, options Container
        // imagePath is used by CRIU to store the actual checkpoint files
        imagePath := ctr.CheckpointPath()
        if options.PreCheckPoint {
-               imagePath = ctr.PreCheckPointPath()
+               imagePath = "../pre-checkpoint"
        }
        // workPath will be used to store dump.log and stats-dump
        workPath := ctr.bundlePath()
```

I will open a PR to fix this.
See #10639 for a fix.
/hold cancel
@rhatdan @cevich PTAL

> What's the reason why the new Fedora image can't also be used?

the Fedora images have a selinux-policy regression that causes all tests to fail

> the Fedora images have a selinux-policy regression that causes all tests to fail

Gotcha.  So the Fedora/Ubuntu image split sounds like a temporary fix.  Would you mind adding a comment such as:

```yaml
    # TODO: At the time of this comment, an selinux-policy regression is blocking use of updated
    # Fedora VM images: <BZ/issue link>
```

That way it can be un-split when the issue/bug is fixed and images need updating again.
addressed the comments :arrow_up:
So we revert this PR or at least the separation of the VMs once selinux-policy gets updated.
LGTM

> So we revert this PR or at least the separation of the VMs once selinux-policy gets updated.

Just the one `.cirrus.yml: use c5521575421149184 for Ubuntu` commit.

But if this takes longer than 30-days, there's a very not-obvious problem WRT image pruning.  I'll open a followup PR so the tests don't all need to be re-run for it (here).

...in case you want to include it in the one commit (would be convenient for reverting), this is what I'm thinking:

```diff
diff --git a/.cirrus.yml b/.cirrus.yml
index a403889c6..026843503 100644
--- a/.cirrus.yml
+++ b/.cirrus.yml
@@ -678,6 +678,8 @@ meta_task:
             ${PRIOR_FEDORA_CACHE_IMAGE_NAME}
             ${UBUNTU_CACHE_IMAGE_NAME}
             ${PRIOR_UBUNTU_CACHE_IMAGE_NAME}
+            "ubuntu-${IMAGE_SUFFIX}"
+            "prior-ubuntu-${IMAGE_SUFFIX}"
         BUILDID: "${CIRRUS_BUILD_ID}"
         REPOREF: "${CIRRUS_REPO_NAME}"
         GCPJSON: ENCRYPTED[3a198350077849c8df14b723c0f4c9fece9ebe6408d35982e7adf2105a33f8e0e166ed3ed614875a0887e1af2b8775f4]
```
thanks for the suggestion.  I've amended the change in the last version

Okay, welp, since we're re-spinning tests I can close my PR then.  Sorry for the noise/churn, I was trying to help avoid needing to re-run all the tests while there's the annoying system-test flake (assuming it's still a thing).

looks like the test doesn't like the last diff:

```

ERROR: (gcloud.compute.images.update) HTTPError 400: Invalid value for field 'image': '"ubuntu-c5348179051806720"'. Must be a match of regex '[a-z](?:[-a-z0-9]{0,61}[a-z0-9])?|[1-9][0-9]{0,19}'
�[1;33mDetected update error for '"ubuntu-c5348179051806720"'�[0m
ERROR: (gcloud.compute.images.update) HTTPError 400: Invalid value for field 'image': '"prior-ubuntu-c5348179051806720"'. Must be a match of regex '[a-z](?:[-a-z0-9]{0,61}[a-z0-9])?|[1-9][0-9]{0,19}'
�[1;33mDetected update error for '"prior-ubuntu-c5348179051806720"'�[0m
�[1;31mERROR: ERROR: Failed to update one or more image timestamps:  "ubuntu-c5348179051806720" "prior-ubuntu-c5348179051806720"�[0m
```
reverted it back, and the CI is smart to turn immediately green :-)
LGTM
/lgtm
